negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The audited port has no FHE-specific Phase 1 fixed fee helper and no Phase 2 base-plus-per-layer FHE pricing with zero validator fees.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-precompile-addresses/src/lib.rs registers FHE_PRECOMPILE as a two-phase precompile.
crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs has generic two-phase and ZK/Persistent Phase 2 helpers, but no FHE Phase 1 or layer-priced Phase 2 helper.
crates/ethereum/payload/src/ritual_async.rs rejects FHE async commitment metadata with the other two-phase families lacking fee parity.

notes: FHE is registered as two-phase metadata, but the actual FHE fee constants and pricing helpers are absent from the audited port and dependency repos.
rationale: The commit adds FHE fee constants and new FHE pricing functions: Phase 1 uses a fixed settlement fee and assumes a job_id-sized output, while Phase 2 computes executor payment from layer count and sets validator fees to zero because they were paid in Phase 1. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: FHE async pricing must charge a fixed Phase 1 executor fee plus normal commitment and 32-byte inclusion fees, and Phase 2 must charge only the FHE execution fee computed as base fee plus per-layer fee with zero commitment and inclusion fees.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call `calculate_fhe_fees_phase1` for a known input length and assert executor fee is `FHE_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI` with inclusion priced for 32 output bytes; call `calculate_fhe_fees_phase2_work(3)` and assert executor fee equals `FHE_BASE_INFERENCE_FEE_WEI + 3 * FHE_LAYER_FEE_WEI` and both validator fee fields are zero.
summary: FHE phase-split pricing is not ported.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call the port fee APIs for FHE Phase 1 and Phase 2 layer counts; the current port has no FHE pricing entrypoint and generic Phase 2 fee calculation rejects 0x0807.
title: FHE pricing is split across phases
